task_completion.description: Log into the 2N Access Commander system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 2N Access Commander web interface (dashboard or user list) loaded in the browser.

task_completion.description: Remove the compromised card for Heather Morrison.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Heather Morrison's user profile or access settings show that the old card (0004521820) is no longer assigned or has been deleted.

task_completion.description: Assign a new sequential card to Heather Morrison.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Heather Morrison's user profile shows a new card assigned, specifically 0004522100 or 0004522101.

task_completion.description: Remove the compromised card for Robert Nakamura.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Robert Nakamura's user profile or access settings show that the old card (0004521821) is no longer assigned or has been deleted.

task_completion.description: Assign a new sequential card to Robert Nakamura.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Robert Nakamura's user profile shows a new card assigned, specifically 0004522101 or 0004522100 (whichever was not assigned to Heather).

task_completion.description: Ensure both affected users remain active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user list or individual profiles for Heather Morrison and Robert Nakamura show their status as 'Active' (e.g., a green toggle, checkmark, or active status badge).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the 2N Access Commander web GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show clicking and typing within the web browser interface, rather than using terminal commands or API scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor to fake the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The card numbers and user details are seen within the actual 2N Access Commander application, not written in a notepad or text editor.

integrity.description: Card numbers were entered into the correct system fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: The new card numbers are visible in the designated 'Card' or 'RFID' input fields or assigned credentials list, not just edited into the page text via developer tools.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The compromised cards belong to Heather Morrison (0004521820) and Robert Nakamura (0004521821). The agent must remove these specific cards and assign new ones starting from 0004522100 sequentially (e.g., 0004522100 to one user, 0004522101 to the other). Both users must remain active.
